name: Reno v. Flores
summary: The Supreme Court applied the stringent 'no set of circumstances' test to a facial challenge to immigration detention regulations, holding that a regulation survives facial challenge unless there is literally no circumstance under which it could lawfully operate.
significance: Justice Sotomayor cited this case for its stricter facial challenge standard — that a challenger must show 'no set of circumstances exists under which the regulation would be valid' — noting this even more demanding test would also be impossible for respondents to satisfy.
